content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a passive optical reflection of external stimuli and an active metacognitive process of reviewing one's own thinking, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to split their function into dual modes: a 'reflection mode' that mirrors inconsistencies between the model's generated outputs and external feedback (analogous to light bouncing off a surface), and a 'reflexion mode' that actively revises the model's implicit problem-solving heuristics stored in dynamic memory.
